steps.title: When does a linear system have no solution?
steps.content: Two lines have no point in common when they are parallel: same slope but different $y$-intercepts. We need to find the choice whose two equations match that pattern.

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $x=3$ is a vertical line and $y=5$ is a horizontal line; they cross at $(3,5)$. One solution.
  • B) $y=6x+6$ has slope $6$ and $y=5x+6$ has slope $5$; different slopes intersect once (here at $(0,6)$). One solution.
  • C) $y=16x+3$ and $y=16x+19$ have the same slope $16$ but different intercepts $3$ and $19$. Parallel, no solution.
  • D) $y=5$ has slope $0$ and $y=5x+5$ has slope $5$; different slopes intersect once (at $(0,5)$). One solution.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
